subject: Databases
title: Automatic Failover
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Automatic Failover.

hook.text: The primary database crashes. A replica detects it, promotes itself to primary, and starts accepting writes in seconds.

buildup.text: Automatic failover minimizes downtime. No human needs to wake up at 2 AM to flip a switch.

discovery.text: Consensus protocols (Raft, Paxos) elect a new leader. The replica with the most up-to-date data wins.

twist.text: Split-brain: two nodes both think they're the leader. Both accept writes. Data diverges. This is catastrophic.

climax.text: Fencing tokens prevent split-brain. The new leader gets a higher token. The old leader's writes are rejected by storage.

punchline.text: Auto-failover: fast recovery. Use fencing tokens to prevent split-brain.

quiz.question: What is split-brain in a database cluster?
options: Two nodes both believe they are the leader and accept conflicting writes simultaneously
The database splits data into two halves for performance
A node intentionally shuts down to save resources
